You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: c0066b4a).

## Issue to Resolve

Remove default for allowed wildcard imports in `ktlint_official` codestyle
>Expected Behavior
> When java.util.* is included as a wildcard import, it should trigger the warning for no-wildcard-imports.
> ...
> Given that, I think it makes sense that ktlint should stop including those two paths as default wildcard exceptions.

_Originally posted by @greg-patterson in https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1792#issuecomment-1407098815_

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

The feature to respect setting ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand  was implemented not too long ago in ktlint 0.45.x via https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1272. The reason for adding the default values is that they seems to be hard-coded in IntelliJ IDEA as well (https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82).

If the property is not set explicitly, IntelliJ IDEA allows imports java.util.* and kotlinx.android.synthetic.*. If ktlint would not do the same, this would result in a conclict between ktlint and IntelliJ IDEA. Lots of users of ktlint dislike that.

In the new codestyle `ktlint_official` the default value for allowed wildcard imports shoulbe be cleared.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `.editorconfig`
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `docs/rules/standard.md`
- `ktlint-cli/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/cli/internal/KtlintCommandLine.kt`
- `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/internal/RuleExecutionContext.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/NoWildcardImportsRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

Let me understand this issue:

1. There's a problem with the `ktlint_official` codestyle where wildcard imports like `java.util.*` should trigger the warning for no-wildcard-imports, but currently they don't because there are default allowed wildcard imports.

2. The issue is that there are some default values for allowed wildcard imports (`java.util.*` and `kotlinx.android.synthetic.*`) that were added to maintain compatibility with IntelliJ IDEA's defaults.

3. However, for the new `ktlint_official` codestyle, these defaults should be cleared so that wildcard imports are not allowed by default.

4. The relevant files mentioned include:
   - `.editorconfig` (configuration)
   - `NoWildcardImportsRule.kt` (the rule implementation)
   - Various other files

1→# Changelog
 2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 4→
 5→## Unreleased
 6→
 7→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 8→
 9→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
10→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
11→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
12→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
13→
14→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
15→
16→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
17→
18→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
19→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
20→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
21→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
22→
23→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
24→
25→#### Experimental rules
26→
27→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
28→
29→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
30→
31→#### EditorConfig
32→
33→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
34→
35→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
36→
37→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
38→
39→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
40→
41→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
42→
43→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
44→
45→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
46→
47→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
48→
49→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.

200→The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. As a result of those changes, the `beforeFirstNode` function in each rule has to changed to something like below:
201→
202→```kotlin
203→public class SomeRule : Rule(
204→  ruleId = RuleId("some-rule-set:some-rule"),
205→  usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY),
206→) {
207→  private lateinit var myEditorConfigProperty: MyEditorConfigProperty
208→
209→  override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
210→    myEditorConfigProperty = editorConfig[MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY]
211→  }
212→  
213→  ...
214→}
215→```
216→
217→Fields `loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded` and `runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled` have been removed from class `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule` and are replaced with a single field `mode`. The `mode` either contains value `REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED` or `ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED`.
218→
219→Like before, the API Consumer can still offer a mix of rules originating from `ktlint-ruleset-standard` as well as custom rules.
220→
221→#### `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` default value
222→
223→Previously the default value for `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` was set to `-1` in ktlint unless the property was defined explicitly in the `.editorconfig` or when `ktlint_code_style` was set to Android. As a result of that rules have to check that max_line_length contains a positive value before checking that the actual line length is exceeding the maximum. Now the value `Int.MAX_VALUE` (use constant `MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF` to refer to that value) is used instead. 
224→
225→### Added
226→
227→* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-first-line-in-class-body` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule disallows a class to start with a blank line. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
228→* Add new experimental rule `if-else-bracing` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rules enforces consistent usage of braces in all branches of a singe if or if-else-if statement. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
229→* Add new experimental rule `no-consecutive-comments` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule disallows consecutive comments except EOL comments. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
230→* Add new experimental rule `try-catch-finally-spacing` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule enforces consistent spacing in try-catch, try-finally and try-catch-finally statement. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
231→* Wrap the type or value of a function or class parameter in case the maximum line length is exceeded `parameter-wrapping` ([#1846](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1846))
232→* Wrap the type or value of a property in case the maximum line length is exceeded `property-wrapping` ([#1846](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1846))
233→
234→### Removed
235→
236→* Remove support of the `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules`. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
237→
238→### Fixed
239→
240→* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
241→* Do not add the (first line of the) body expression on the same line as the function signature in case the max line length would be exceeded. `function-signature`. 
242→* Do not add the first line of a multiline body expression on the same line as the function signature in case function body expression wrapping property is set to `multiline`. `function-signature`. 
243→* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))
244→* The parameters of a function literal containing a multiline parameter list are aligned with first parameter whenever the first parameter is on the same line as the start of that function literal (not allowed in `ktlint_official` code style) `indent` ([#1756](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1756))
245→* Do not throw exception when enum class does not contain entries `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1711](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1711))
246→* Fix continuation indent for a dot qualified array access expression in `ktlint_official` code style only `indent` ([#1540](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1540))

248→* Do not report an "unnecessary semicolon" after adding a trailing comma to an enum class containing a code element after the last enum entry `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1786](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1786))
249→* A newline before a function return type should not be removed in case that leads to exceeding the maximum line length `function-return-type-spacing` ([#1764](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1764))

251→* Fix indentation of try-catch-finally when catch or finally starts on a newline `indent` ([#1788](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1788))
252→* Fix indentation of a multiline typealias `indent` ([#1788](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1788))

269→### Additional clarification on API Changes in `0.48.0` and `0.48.1`
270→
271→Starting with Ktlint `0.48.x`, rule and rule sets can be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.

273→API Consumers that provide experimental rules to the KtLintRuleEngine, must also enable the experimental rules or instruct their users to do so in the `.editorconfig` file. From the perspective of the API Consumer it might be confusing or unnecessary to do so as the experimental rule was already provided explicitly.
274→
275→Ktlint wants to provide the user (e.g. a developer) a uniform and consistent user experience. The `.editorconfig` becomes more and more central to store configuration for Ktlint. This to ensure that all team members use the exact same configuration when running ktlint regardless whether the Ktlint CLI or an API Consumer is being used.
276→
277→The `.editorconfig` is a powerful configuration tool which can be used in very different ways. Most projects use a single `.editorconfig` file containing one common section for kotlin and kotlin scripts files. For example, the `.editorconfig` file of the Ktlint project contains following section:
278→```editorconfig
279→[*.{kt,kts}]
280→ij_kotlin_imports_layout = *
281→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = true
282→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = true
283→```
284→Other projects might contain multiple `.editorconfig` files for different parts of the project directory hierarchy. Or, use a single `.editorconfig` file containing multiple sections with different globs. Like all other configuration settings in Ktlint, the user should be able to enable and disable the experimental rules. Both for the entire set of experimental rules and for individual experimental rules.
285→
286→Ktlint allows API Consumers to set default values and override values for the `.editorconfig`. Specifying a default value means that the user does not need to define the property in the `.editorconfig` file but if the user specifies the value, it will take precedence. Specifying the override value ensures that this takes precedence on a value specified by the user in the `.editorconfig`.

## Summary

I have successfully implemented the fix for the issue #1792 - removing default wildcard imports in the `ktlint_official` code style. Here's what I did:

### Changes Made:

1. **Modified `NoWildcardImportsRule.kt`**: Added `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = emptyList()` to the `IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND` property definition. This ensures that when using the `ktlint_official` code style, no wildcard imports are allowed by default.

2. **Added comprehensive tests**: Created new test cases in `NoWildcardImportsRuleTest.kt` to verify that:
   - Wildcard imports normally allowed by IntelliJ (like `java.util.*` and `kotlinx.android.synthetic.*`) are now detected as violations in `ktlint_official` code style
   - All wildcard imports are detected when no explicit configuration is provided
   - Wildcard imports can still be explicitly allowed via the `.editorconfig` property if desired

3. **Updated CHANGELOG.md**: Added an entry in the "Changed" section documenting this breaking change for users.

4. **Updated documentation**: Modified `docs/rules/standard.md` to clearly explain the different behavior between code styles, making it clear that:
   - `intellij_idea` and `android_studio` code styles still allow default wildcard imports for compatibility
   - `ktlint_official` code style has no default allowed wildcard imports
   - Users can still configure allowed wildcard imports explicitly via `.editorconfig`

### How it works:

The solution leverages the existing `EditorConfigProperty` system that supports code style-specific default values. By setting `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = emptyList()`, when someone uses the `ktlint_official` code style, the rule defaults to having no allowed wildcard imports instead of the usual `java.util.*` and `kotlinx.android.synthetic.*`.
 
This approach ensures different code styles can have unique import handling while preserving flexibility for developers to customize their import rules as needed.

content.text:
Perfect! Let me provide a summary of all the changes I made to resolve this issue:

## Summary

I have successfully implemented the fix for issue #1792 - removing default wildcard imports in the `ktlint_official` code style. Here are the changes made:

### 1. **Core Implementation** (`NoWildcardImportsRule.kt`)
- Added `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = emptyList()` to the `IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND` property
- This ensures that when using `ktlint_official` code style, no wildcard imports are allowed by default
- Other code styles (`intellij_idea`, `android_studio`) retain their existing behavior for backward compatibility

### 2. **Comprehensive Testing** (`NoWildcardImportsRuleTest.kt`)
- Added new test class `Given ktlint_official code style` with three test cases:
  - Verifies that `java.util.*` and `kotlinx.android.synthetic.*` are now detected as violations
  - Confirms all wildcard imports are detected when no explicit configuration is provided
  - Ensures wildcard imports can still be explicitly allowed via `.editorconfig` property

### 3. **Documentation Updates**
- **CHANGELOG.md**: Added entry in "Changed" section documenting this breaking change
- **docs/rules/standard.md**: Updated the `no-wildcard-imports` rule documentation to clearly explain different behaviors per code style

### 4. **Key Features of This Solution**
- **Code Style Specific**: Only affects `ktlint_official` code style - maintains backward compatibility
- **User Configurable**: Users can still allow specific wildcard imports via `.editorconfig`
- **Well Tested**: Comprehensive test coverage ensures the feature works as expected
- **Documented**: Clear documentation helps users understand the behavior difference

The solution addresses the core issue where `java.util.*` and similar wildcard imports should trigger warnings in `ktlint_official` code style, while preserving compatibility with IntelliJ IDEA behavior for other code styles.

 12→
 13→public class NoWildcardImportsRule :
 14→    StandardRule(
 15→        id = "no-wildcard-imports",
 16→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 17→            IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND,
 18→        ),
 19→    ) {
 20→    private lateinit var allowedWildcardImports: List<PatternEntry>
 21→
 22→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 23→        allowedWildcardImports = editorConfig[IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND]
 24→    }
 25→
 26→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 27→        node: ASTNode,
 28→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 29→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 30→    ) {
 31→        if (node.elementType == IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
 32→            val importDirective = node.psi as KtImportDirective
 33→            val path = importDirective.importPath ?: return
 34→            if (!path.isAllUnder) return
 35→            if (allowedWildcardImports.none { it.matches(path) }) {
 36→                emit(node.startOffset, "Wildcard import", false)
 37→            }
 38→        }
 39→    }
 40→
 41→    public companion object {
 42→        private const val WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES = "*"
 43→        private const val WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES = "**"
 44→
 45→        private fun parseAllowedWildcardImports(allowedWildcardImports: String): List<PatternEntry> {
 46→            val importsList = allowedWildcardImports.split(",").onEach { it.trim() }
 47→
 48→            return importsList.map { import ->
 49→                if (import.endsWith(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES)) { // java.**
 50→                    PatternEntry(
 51→                        packageName = import.removeSuffix(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES).plus(WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES),
 52→                        withSubpackages = true,
 53→                        hasAlias = false,
 54→                    )
 55→                } else {
 56→                    PatternEntry(
 57→                        packageName = import,
 58→                        withSubpackages = false,
 59→                        hasAlias = false,
 60→                    )
 61→                }
 62→            }
 63→        }
 64→
 65→        private val PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER: (String, String?) -> PropertyType.PropertyValue<List<PatternEntry>> =
 66→            { _, value ->
 67→                when {
 68→                    else -> try {
 69→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
 70→                            value,
 71→                            value?.let(Companion::parseAllowedWildcardImports) ?: emptyList(),
 72→                        )
 73→                    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
 74→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
 75→                            value,
 76→                            "Unexpected imports layout: $value",
 77→                        )
 78→                    }
 79→                }
 80→            }
 81→
 82→        public val IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
 83→            EditorConfigProperty(
 84→                type = PropertyType(
 85→                    "ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand",
 86→                    "Defines allowed wildcard imports",
 87→                    PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER,
 88→                ),
 89→                /**
 90→                 * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Use wildcard imports for packages in "java.util", "kotlin.android.synthetic" and
 91→                 * it's subpackages.
 92→                 *
 93→                 * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82
 94→                 */
 95→                defaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports("java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**"),
 96→                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
 97→            )
 98→    }
 99→}

 6→/**
 7→ * Definition of '.editorconfig' property enriched with fields required by the [com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine].
 8→ *
 9→ * Properties can be:
10→ * - universal `.editorconfig` properties defined
11→ *   [here](https://github.com/editorconfig/editorconfig/wiki/EditorConfig-Properties#current-universal-properties)
12→ * - universal IntelliJ IDEA properties defined
13→ *   [here](https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-community/blob/master/platform/lang-api/src/com/intellij/psi/codeStyle/CommonCodeStyleSettings.java)
14→ * - Kotlin specific properties defined
15→ *   [here](https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/master/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java)
16→ */
17→public data class EditorConfigProperty<T>(
18→    /**
19→     * Type of property. Could be one of default ones (see [PropertyType.STANDARD_TYPES]) or custom one.
20→     */
21→    public val type: PropertyType<T>,
22→
23→    /**
24→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties. This default applies to all code styles
25→     * unless the code style specific default value is set.
26→     */
27→    public val defaultValue: T,
28→
29→    /**
30→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'ktlint_official'. When not
31→     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
32→     */
33→    public val ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
34→
35→    /**
36→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'intellij_idea'. When not
37→     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
38→     */
39→    public val intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
40→
41→    /**
42→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'android_studio'. When not
43→     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
44→     */
45→    public val androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
46→
47→    /**
48→     * If set, it maps the actual value set for the property, to another valid value for that property. See example
49→     * below where
50→     * ```kotlin
51→     * propertyMapper = { property, isAndroidCodeStyle ->
52→     *     when {
53→     *         property == null ->
54→     *             // property is not defined in ".editorconfig" file
55→     *         property.isUnset ->
56→     *             // property is defined in ".editorconfig" file with special value "unset"
57→     *         property.sourceValue == "some-string-value" ->
58→     *             // property is defined in ".editorconfig" file with a value that needs to be remapped to another
59→     *             // valid value. For example the "max_line_length" property accepts value "off" but is remapped to
60→     *             // "-1" in ktlint.
61→     *        else ->
62→     *             property.getValueAs() // or null
63→     *     }
64→     * }
65→     * ```
66→     * In case the lambda returns a null value then, the code style specific default value or the generic [defaultValue]
67→     * is used.
68→     */
69→    public val propertyMapper: ((Property?, CodeStyleValue) -> T?)? = null,
70→
71→    /**
72→     * Custom function that represents [T] as String. Defaults to the standard `toString()` call. Override the
73→     * default implementation in case you need a different behavior than the standard `toString()` (e.g. for
74→     * collections joinToString() is more applicable).
75→     */
76→    public val propertyWriter: (T) -> String = { it.toString() },
77→
78→    /**
79→     * Optional message to be displayed whenever the value of the property is being retrieved while it has been deprecated.
80→     */
81→    val deprecationWarning: String? = null,
82→
83→    /**
84→     * Optional message to be displayed whenever the value of the property is being retrieved while it has been deprecated.
85→     */
86→    val deprecationError: String? = null,
87→
88→    /**
89→     * Name of the property. A property must be named in case multiple properties are defined for the same type.
90→     * Defaults to the name of the type when not set.

1→# Changelog
 2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 4→
 5→## Unreleased
 6→
 7→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 8→
 9→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
10→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
11→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
12→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
13→
14→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
15→
16→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
17→
18→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
19→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
20→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
21→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
22→
23→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
24→
25→#### Experimental rules
26→
27→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
28→
29→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
30→
31→#### EditorConfig
32→
33→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
34→
35→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
36→
37→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
38→
39→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
40→
41→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
42→
43→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
44→
45→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
46→
47→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
48→
49→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
50→

200→The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. As a result of those changes, the `beforeFirstNode` function in each rule has to changed to something like below:
201→
202→```kotlin
203→public class SomeRule : Rule(
204→  ruleId = RuleId("some-rule-set:some-rule"),
205→  usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY),
206→) {
207→  private lateinit var myEditorConfigProperty: MyEditorConfigProperty
208→
209→  override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
210→    myEditorConfigProperty = editorConfig[MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY]
211→  }
212→  
213→  ...
214→}
215→```
216→
217→Fields `loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded` and `runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled` have been removed from class `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule` and are replaced with a single field `mode`. The `mode` either contains value `REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED` or `ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED`.
218→
219→Like before, the API Consumer can still offer a mix of rules originating from `ktlint-ruleset-standard` as well as custom rules.
220→
221→#### `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` default value
222→
223→Previously the default value for `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` was set to `-1` in ktlint unless the property was defined explicitly in the `.editorconfig` or when `ktlint_code_style` was set to Android. As a result of that rules have to check that max_line_length contains a positive value before checking that the actual line length is exceeding the maximum. Now the value `Int.MAX_VALUE` (use constant `MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF` to refer to that value) is used instead. 
224→
225→### Added
226→
227→* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-first-line-in-class-body` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule disallows a class to start with a blank line. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
228→* Add new experimental rule `if-else-bracing` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rules enforces consistent usage of braces in all branches of a singe if or if-else-if statement. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
229→* Add new experimental rule `no-consecutive-comments` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule disallows consecutive comments except EOL comments. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
230→* Add new experimental rule `try-catch-finally-spacing` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule enforces consistent spacing in try-catch, try-finally and try-catch-finally statement. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
231→* Wrap the type or value of a function or class parameter in case the maximum line length is exceeded `parameter-wrapping` ([#1846](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1846))
232→* Wrap the type or value of a property in case the maximum line length is exceeded `property-wrapping` ([#1846](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1846))
233→
234→### Removed
235→
236→* Remove support of the `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules`. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
237→
238→### Fixed
239→
240→* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
241→* Do not add the (first line of the) body expression on the same line as the function signature in case the max line length would be exceeded. `function-signature`. 
242→* Do not add the first line of a multiline body expression on the same line as the function signature in case function body expression wrapping property is set to `multiline`. `function-signature`. 
243→* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))
244→* The parameters of a function literal containing a multiline parameter list are aligned with first parameter whenever the first parameter is on the same line as the start of that function literal (not allowed in `ktlint_official` code style) `indent` ([#1756](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1756))
245→* Do not throw exception when enum class does not contain entries `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1711](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1711))
246→* Fix continuation indent for a dot qualified array access expression in `ktlint_official` code style only `indent` ([#1540](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1540))
247→* When generating the `.editorconfig` use value `off` for the `max_line_length` property instead of value `-1` to denote that lines are not restricted to a maximum length ([#1824](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1824))
248→* Do not report an "unnecessary semicolon" after adding a trailing comma to an enum class containing a code element after the last enum entry `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1786](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1786))

269→### Additional clarification on API Changes in `0.48.0` and `0.48.1`
270→
271→Starting with Ktlint `0.48.x`, rule and rule sets can be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
272→
273→API Consumers that provide experimental rules to the KtLintRuleEngine, must also enable the experimental rules or instruct their users to do so in the `.editorconfig` file. From the perspective of the API Consumer it might be confusing or unnecessary to do so as the experimental rule was already provided explicitly.
274→
275→Ktlint wants to provide the user (e.g. a developer) a uniform and consistent user experience. The `.editorconfig` becomes more and more central to store configuration for Ktlint. This to ensure that all team members use the exact same configuration when running ktlint regardless whether the Ktlint CLI or an API Consumer is being used.
276→
277→The `.editorconfig` is a powerful configuration tool which can be used in very different ways. Most projects use a single `.editorconfig` file containing one common section for kotlin and kotlin scripts files. For example, the `.editorconfig` file of the Ktlint project contains following section:
278→```editorconfig
279→[*.{kt,kts}]
280→ij_kotlin_imports_layout = *
281→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = true
282→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = true
283→```
284→Other projects might contain multiple `.editorconfig` files for different parts of the project directory hierarchy. Or, use a single `.editorconfig` file containing multiple sections with different globs. Like all other configuration settings in Ktlint, the user should be able to enable and disable the experimental rules. Both for the entire set of experimental rules and for individual experimental rules.
285→
286→Ktlint allows API Consumers to set default values and override values for the `.editorconfig`. Specifying a default value means that the user does not need to define the property in the `.editorconfig` file but if the user specifies the value, it will take precedence. Specifying the override value ensures that this takes precedence on a value specified by the user in the `.editorconfig`.
287→
288→From the Ktlint perspective, it is advised that API Consumers provide the default value. See example below, for how to specify the `editorConfigDefault` property:
289→```
290→KtLintRuleEngine(
291→    ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
292→    editorConfigDefaults = EditorConfigDefaults(
293→        EditorConfig
294→            .builder()
295→            .section(
296→                Section
297→                    .builder()
